title: Trump's 15-Point Peace Plan Delivered to Iran via Pakistani Intermediaries
detail: Iran received President Trump's 15-point peace plan, delivered by intermediaries through Pakistan, the Associated Press reported. The plan centers on sanctions relief, civilian nuclear cooperation, missile program limits, rollback of Iran's nuclear program and IAEA monitoring, and opening the Strait of Hormuz to shipping. Trump declared the US is 'in negotiations right now' and that Iran had offered 'a very significant prize' related to the strait, while also claiming the US had already won the war. Trump said he backed off from threatening strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure 'based on the fact we're negotiating.'

title: Iranian Missile Strikes Tel Aviv After Overnight Israeli Strikes on Iran
detail: Iran launched a fresh round of missile and drone strikes toward Israel and Persian Gulf states. An Iranian missile landed in Tel Aviv on Tuesday morning after Israeli airstrikes struck multiple Iranian sites overnight. Three people were injured, including one critically, by a rocket impact near Mahanayim Junction in northern Israel from a Hezbollah barrage of approximately 30 rockets. Israel said its forces were simultaneously striking Iran and Lebanon, including targets in Beirut.

title: Israeli Strike on Beirut Apartment Kills Three Including Child
detail: An Israeli airstrike on a residential apartment building in Bchamoun in the Beirut suburbs killed at least three people, including a three-year-old girl, according to the Lebanese Ministry of Public Health. Five others were wounded. Israel's Defense Minister Katz declared Israel would establish a 'security zone' in southern Lebanon stretching to the Litani River, roughly 30km north of the border.

title: Saudi Arabia and UAE Reportedly Losing Patience, May Join Strikes on Iran
detail: Saudi Arabia and the UAE were reportedly 'losing patience' with Iranian attacks on their energy infrastructure and airports and could join attacks on Iran, Bloomberg reported, citing several people with knowledge of the situation. Iran struck a fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport, sparking a fire. The White House threatened Iran would be 'hit harder than they have ever been hit before' if it failed to accept the military reality.
